rationale: Both papers are tightly aligned on financially equivalent price framing and perception-based mechanisms: the citing paper studies DPF vs IPF effects on perceived expensiveness/choice, while the cited paper studies equivalent discount reframing effects on perceived discount depth and buying responses. Given the citing note explicitly positions its contribution as extending prior price-presentation research, this 2018 JMR article is plausibly a direct theoretical/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention.
